lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: I am still waiting for your letter of recommendation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the letter you gave me.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Oh now come on. You have it with you!

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: That was foolish. Take this one and don't lose it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll try and get it.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: I am sure you won't get any problems. Speak to the Curator of Varrock's museum.

sections.title: Using an unstamped letter on the examiner

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: I don't really want this back until it's been stamped!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have lost the letter of recommendation that you stamped for me.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Yes, I saw you drop it as you walked off last time. Here it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I see you still have that letter I stamped for you. Why don't you run it back to the Examiner at the Exam Centre?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have another letter here; could you stamp it for me again?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: You've already given me one of those! But... ok, give it here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Using the stamped letter on the curator

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: No I don't want it back thank you, you'll need to take that back to the examiner at the Digsite.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been given this letter by the examiner at the digsite; can you stamp it for me?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: What have we here? A letter of recommendation indeed!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Normally, I wouldn't do this... but in this instance I don't see why not.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: The letter here says your name is [player name]. Well, [player name], I wouldn't normally do this for just anyone, but as you did us such a great service with the Shield of Arrav I don't see why not. Run this letter back to

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: the Examiner to begin your adventure into the world of Earth Sciences. Enjoy your studies, Student!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: There you go; good luck student. Be sure to come back and show me your certificates. I would like to see how you get on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I will. Thanks; see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Ah hello there! I am the resident lecturer on antiquities and artefacts. I also set the Earth Sciences exams.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Earth Sciences?

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: That is right dear, the world of Gielinor holds many wonders beneath its surface. Students come to me to take exams so that they may join in on the archaeological dig going on just north of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if they don't pass the exams they can't dig at all?

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: That's right! We have to make sure that students know enough to be able to dig safely and not damage the artefacts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I take an exam?

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Are you sure you want to? Our exams are serious business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Very well. You can if you get this letter stamped by the Curator of Varrock's museum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why's that then?

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Because he is a very knowledgeable man and employs our archaeological expert. I'm sure he knows a lot about your exploits and can judge whether you'd make a good archaeologist or not.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Besides, the museum contributes funds to the dig.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why are you writing the letter? Shouldn't he?

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: He's also a very busy man, so I write the letters and he justs stamps them if he approves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see. I'll ask him if he'll approve me, and bring my stamped letter back here. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On second thoughts, I'd better not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You could gain much with an understanding of the world below.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Hello again. Are you ready for another shot at the exam?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not at the moment.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, take your time if you wish.

sections.title: Using another stamped letter on the examiner

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You've already shown me that! Get digging!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here is the stamped letter you asked for.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Good good, we will begin the exam...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, we will start with the first exam: Earth Sciences level 1 - Beginner.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Question 1 - Earth Sciences overview. Can you tell me what Earth Sciences is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The study of the earth, its contents and history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The study of gardening, planting and fruiting vegetation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The study of planets and the history of the worlds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The combination of archaeology and vegetarianism.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, next question...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Earth Sciences level 1, question 2 - Eligibility. Can you tell me which people are allowed to use the digsite?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Magic users, miners and their escorts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Professors, students and workmen only.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Local residents, contractors and small pink fish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All that have passed the appropriate Earth Sciences exam.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, next question...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Earth Sciences level 1, question 3 - Health and safety. Can you tell me the proper safety points when working on a digsite?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heat-resistant clothing to be worn at all times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rubber chickens to be worn on the head at all times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gloves and boots to be worn at all times; proper tools must be used.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Protective clothing to be worn; tools kept away from site.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: That concludes the exam for Earth Sciences level 1.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Let's see how you did...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Oh deary me! This is appalling, none correct at all! I suggest you go and study properly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Why don't you use the resources here? There are books and the researchers, and you could even ask other students who are also studying for these exams.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You got 1 question correct. Better luck next time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh bother!

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Do some more research. I'm sure other students could help you out.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You got 2 questions correct. Not bad, just a little more revision needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You got all of the questions correct! Well done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! Excellent!

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You have now passed the Earth Sciences level 1 general exam. Here is your certificate to prove it. You also get a decent trowel to dig with. Of course, you'll want to get studying for your next exam now!

sections.title: The student in the green top

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with the Earth Sciences exams at all?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Well... Maybe I will if you help me with something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I have lost my recent good find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does it look like?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Err... Like an animal skull!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's not too helpful, there are lots of those around here; can you remember where you last had it?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: It was around here for sure. Maybe someone picked it up?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll have a look for you.

sections.title: Returning to the student without the skull

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's the study going?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Very well thanks. Have you found my animal skull yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No sorry, not yet.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Oh well, I am sure it's been picked up. Couldn't you try looking through some pockets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this your animal skull?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Oh wow! You've found it! Thank you so much. I'll be glad to tell you what I know about the exam.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: The study of Earth Sciences is: The study of the earth, its contents and history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll remember that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need more help with the exam.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Well ok, this is what I have learned since I last spoke to you...

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: The study of Earth Sciences is: The study of the earth, its contents and history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll remember that.

sections.title: The student in the purple skirt

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with the Earth Sciences exams at all?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I can if you help me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I do that?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I have lost my teddy bear. He was my lucky mascot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where you dropped him?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Well, I was doing a lot of walking that day... Oh yes, that's right - we were studying ceramics in fact, near the edge of the digsite.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I found some pottery that seemed to match the design on those large urns.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I was in the process of checking this out, and when we got back to the centre my lucky mascot had gone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Leave it to me, I'll find it.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Oh great! Thanks!

sections.title: Returning to the student without the teddy bear

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's the study going?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Very well thanks. Have you found my lucky mascot yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No sorry, not yet.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I'm sure it's just outside the digsite somewhere...

sections.title: Searching the bush for the teddy

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, something has been dropped here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guess what I found.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Hey! My lucky mascot! Thanks ever so much. Let me help you with those questions now.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: The proper health and safety points are: Leather gloves and boots to be worn at all times; proper tools must be used.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks for your advice.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am stuck on some more exam questions.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Okay, I'll tell you my latest notes...

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: The proper health and safety points are: Leather gloves and boots to be worn at all times; proper tools must be used.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks for your advice.

sections.title: The student in the orange shirt

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with the Earth Sciences exams at all?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I can't do anything unless I find my special cup.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your what?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: My special cup. I won it for a particularly good find last month.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, right. So if I find it you'll help me?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I sure will!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas where it may be?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: All I remember is that I was working near the tents when I lost it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can do.

sections.title: Returning to the student without the cup

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's the study going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm getting there. Have you found my special cup yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No sorry, not yet.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Oh dear, I hope it didn't fall into the stream; I might never find it again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, who are you?

lines.speaker: Panning guide
lines.text: Hello, I am the panning guide. I teach students how to pan in these waters. They're not permitted to do so until after they've had training and, of course, they must be invited to pan here too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do I become invited then?

lines.speaker: Panning guide
lines.text: I'm not supposed to let people pan here unless they have permission from the authorities first. Mind you, I could let you have a go if you're willing to do me a favour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Panning guide
lines.text: Well, to be honest, what I would really like is... a nice cup of tea!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've some here that you can have.

lines.speaker: Panning guide
lines.text: Ah! Lovely! You can't beat a good cuppa... You're free to pan all you want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've some here that you can have.

lines.speaker: Panning guide
lines.text: Urg! Is that what you call tea? Oh well, I suppose it will do; you can pan all you want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tea?!

lines.speaker: Panning guide
lines.text: Absolutely, I'm parched!

lines.speaker: Panning guide
lines.text: If you could bring me one of those, I would be more than willing to let you pan here. I usually get some from Varrock but I'm busy at the moment.

sections.title: Attempting to pan with a full tray

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already have a full panning tray; perhaps I should search it first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look what I found!

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Excellent! I'm so happy. Let me now help you with your exams...

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: The people eligible to use the digsite are: All that have passed the appropriate Earth Sciences exams.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are more exam questions I'm stuck on.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Hey, I'll tell you what I've learned. That may help.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: The people eligible to use the digsite are: All that have passed the appropriate Earth Sciences exams.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information.

sections.title: Talking to the researcher before completing the first exam

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Hello there. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to pass the Earth Sciences exams so I can dig at the site north of here.

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: A good goal to work towards, and it should prove rewarding for you. So where are you starting your studies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not quite sure.

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Well, you could try looking in the Earth Sciences section of the shelves over there, although the other students may have beaten you to it.

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: You could even ask the students themselves. I'm sure I saw a group of them on their way to the digsite earlier today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: If you need something identified or are not sure about something, give it to Terry; he’s the archaeological expert in the next room.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about the tools an archaeologist uses?

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Of course! Let's see now... Rock picks are for splitting rocks or scraping away soil; you can get one from a cupboard in the Education Centre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about sample jars?

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: I think you'll find them scattered about pretty much everywhere, but I know you can get one from a cupboard somewhere in the Education Centre, just like the rock pick!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, what about a specimen brush?

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: We have a bit of a shortage of those at the moment. You could try borrowing one from a workman on the site... but I don't think they'd give it willingly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like I'll need to be sneaky to get one of those, then... Okay - trowel?

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Ahh... that you must earn by passing your exams! The examiner holds those.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: If you need something identified or are not sure about something, give it to Terry; he's the archaeological expert in the next room.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahh, ok thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you!

sections.title: Talking to the researcher after completing the first exam

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Oh, hello there. Congratulations on passing your first exam, I saw your performance, well done. Are you going to go for the next level?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! I should probably talk to those students again; they were very helpful.

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Glad to hear it!

sections.title: The student in the green top

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need more help with the exam.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Well ok, this is what I have learned since I last spoke to you...

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Correct rock pick usage: Always handle with care; strike the rock cleanly on its cleaving point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll remember that.

sections.title: The student in the purple skirt

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am stuck on some more exam questions.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Okay, I'll tell you my latest notes...

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Finds handling: Finds must be carefully handled, and gloves worn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks for your advice.

sections.title: The student in the orange shirt

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are more exam questions I'm stuck on.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Hey, I'll tell you what I've learned. That may help.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Correct sample transportation: Samples taken in rough form; kept only in sealed containers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there! I passed my second Earth Sciences exam.

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Well done! Are you planning on going for level 3?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! Someday I'll know as much as you! Now, where did I leave those students....

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Have fun and good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Hi there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am ready for the next exam.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, this is the next part of the Earth Sciences exam: Earth Sciences level 2 - Intermediate.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Question 1 - Sample transportation. Can you tell me how we transport samples?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Samples cut and cleaned before transportation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Samples ground and suspended in an acid solution.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Samples to be given to the melon-collecting monkey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Samples taken in rough form; kept only in sealed containers.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, next question...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Earth Sciences level 2, question 2 - Handling of finds. What is the proper way to handle finds?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Finds must not be handled by anyone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Finds must be carefully handled, and gloves worn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Finds to be given to the site workmen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drop them on the floor and jump on them.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, next question...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Earth Sciences level 2, question 3 - Rock pick usage. Can you tell me the proper use for a rock pick?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Strike rock repeatedly until powdered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rock pick must be used flat and with a strong force.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Always handle with care; strike cleanly on its cleaving point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rock picks are to be used to milk cows on a rainy morning.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, that concludes the level 2 Earth Sciences exam.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Let me add up your total..

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: No, no, no! This will not do. They are all wrong; start again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: More studying for you my [boy/girl/friend].

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You got 1 question correct. At least it's a start.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Get out and explore the digsite, talk to people and learn!

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You got 2 questions correct. Not too bad, but you can do better...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nearly got it.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You got all the questions correct, well done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I'm getting good at this.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You have now passed the Earth Sciences level 2 intermediate exam. Here is your certificate. Of course, you'll want to get studying for your next exam now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am stuck on a question.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Well, well, have you not been doing any studies? I am not giving you the answers, talk to the other students and remember the answers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I didn't mean to disturb you.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Oh, no problem at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost my trowel.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Deary me... That was a good one as well. It's a good job I have another. Here you go...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Really? Look in your backpack and make sure first.

sections.title: The student in the green top

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need more help with the exam.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Well ok, this is what I have learned since I last spoke to you...

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Specimen brush use: Brush carefully and slowly using short strokes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll remember that. Thanks for all your help.

sections.title: The student in the purple skirt

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Oh, hi again. Did you bring me the opal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't found one yet.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Oh well, tell me when you do. Remember that they can be found around the digsite; perhaps try panning the river.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Oh, hi again. Did you bring me the opal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would an opal look like this by any chance?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Wow, great, you've found one. This will look beautiful set in my necklace. Thanks for that; now I'll tell you what I know...

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Sample preparation: Samples cleaned, and carried only in specimen jars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks for your advice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am stuck on some more exam questions.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Okay, I'll tell you my latest notes...

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Sample preparation: Samples cleaned, and carried only in specimen jars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks for your advice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: What, you want more help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... Yes please!

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Well... It's going to cost you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well how much?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I'll tell you what I would like: a precious stone. I don't find many of them. My favourites are opals; they are beautiful.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Just like me! Tee hee hee!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... OK I'll see what I can do, but I'm not sure where I'd get one.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Well, I have seen people get them from panning occasionally.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK, I'll see what I can turn up for you.

sections.title: The student in the orange shirt

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are more exam questions I'm stuck on.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Hey, I'll tell you what I've learned. That may help.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: The proper technique for handling bones is: Handle bones carefully and keep them away from other samples.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did it! Level 3 Earth Sciences!

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Bravo! Well done indeed! What are your plans now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm not really sure; I guess I should see what I can find around the site.

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: I hear that the third level digs yield the best artefacts for display; Terry, the archaeological expert, has found many good things there that he's excited about.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Well, what are you doing here? Get digging!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Ah, hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am ready for the last exam...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Attention, this is the final part of the Earth Sciences exam: Earth Sciences level 3 - Advanced.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Question 1 - Sample preparation. Can you tell me how we prepare samples?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Samples may be mixed together safely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Samples cleaned, and carried only in specimen jars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sample types catalogued and carried by hand only.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Samples to be spread thickly with mashed banana.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, next question...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Earth Sciences level 3, question 2 - Specimen brush use. What is the proper way to use a specimen brush?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brush quickly using a wet brush.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brush carefully and slowly using short strokes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dipped in glue and stuck to a sheep's back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brush quickly and with force.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, next question...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Earth Sciences level 3, question 3 - Advanced techniques. Can you describe the technique for handling bones?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bones must not be taken from the digsite.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Feed to hungry dogs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bones to be ground and tested for mineral content.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Handle bones very carefully and keep them away from other samples.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Okay, that concludes the level 3 Earth Sciences exam.

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Let me add up the results..

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: I cannot believe this! Absolutely none right at all. I doubt you did any research before you took this exam...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... Yes... Erm... I think I had better go and revise first!

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You got 1 question correct. Try harder!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh bother!

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You got 2 questions correct. A little more study and you will pass it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm nearly there...

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: You got all the questions correct, well done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hooray!

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Congratulations! You have now passed the Earth Sciences level 3 exam. Here is your level 3 certificate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can dig wherever I want now!

lines.speaker: Examiner
lines.text: Perhaps you should use your newfound skills to find an artefact on the digsite that will impress the archaeological expert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take a look at this talisman.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Unusual... This object doesn't appear right...

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Hmmm...

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: I wonder... Let me check my guide... Could it be? Surely not!

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: From the markings on it, it seems to be a ceremonial ornament to a god named...

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: ...Zaros? I haven't heard much about him before. This is a great discovery; we know very little of the pagan gods that people worshipped.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: There is some strange writing embossed upon it - it says, 'Zaros will return and wreak his vengeance upon Zamorak the pretender.'

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: I wonder what it means by that? Some silly superstition, probably.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Still, I wonder what this is doing around here. I'll tell you what; as you have found this, I will allow you to use the private dig shafts.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: You obviously have a keen eye. Take this letter and give it to one of the workmen, and they will allow you to use them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Good day to you. My name is Terry Balando; I am an expert on digsite finds.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: I am employed by the museum in Varrock to oversee all finds at this digsite. Anything you find must be reported to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, ok. If I find anything of interest I will bring it here.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Can I help you at all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the letter you gave me.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: No you haven't! You have one in your pack!

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: No you haven't! You have one in your bank!

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: I can't believe it! I go to all the effort of writing you a letter of recommendation and you lose it! Here, I'll write another... Don't lose it again!

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: There's no point in giving me this back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, have a look at this...

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: I give permission... blah de blah... etc. Okay, that's all in order; you may use the mineshaft now. I'll hang onto this scroll, shall I?

sections.title: Using a second invitation on a workman

lines.speaker: Digsite workman
lines.text: What on earth are you giving me another scroll for?

sections.title: Talking to Terry after giving the workman the invitation

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Oh, hello. I hope you're getting on well in the dig shafts.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Can I help you at all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It says, 'I give permission for the bearer... to use the mine shafts on site. - signed Terrance Balando, Archaeological Expert, City of Varrock.'

sections.title: Using a rope on the winch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I think I could fit down here. I need something to help me get all the way down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, there's a room past these bricks. If I could move them out of the way then I could find out what's inside. Maybe there's someone around here who can help...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: Well, well... I have a visitor. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been invited to research here.

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: Indeed, you must be someone special to be allowed down here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where to find a specimen jar?

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: Hmmm, let me think... Nope, can't help you there I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have things to do...

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: Of course, don't let me keep you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am not sure really.

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: A miner without a clue - how funny!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to get rich, rich, rich!

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: Oh, well, don't forget that wealth and riches aren't everything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you move a large pile of rocks?

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: There used to be this chap that worked in the other shaft. He was working on an explosive chemical mixture to be used for clearing blocked areas underground.

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: He left in a hurry one day; something in the shaft scared him to death, but he didn't say what.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: Rumour has it he'd been writing a book on his chemical mixture. I'm not sure what goes in it but I'm sure you'll find the stuff he was using scattered around the digsite. He left so quickly he didn't take anything with

lines.speaker: Doug Deeping
lines.text: him. In fact, I still have a chest key he gave me to look after; perhaps it's more useful to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's ok, I already have one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mmmm... The lid is shut tight; I'll have to find something to lever it off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's closed tight.

sections.title: Using a trowel on the barrel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! It's opened.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't pick this up with my bare hands! I'll need something to put it in. It looks and smells rather dangerous though, so it'll need to be something small and capable of containing dangerous chemicals.

sections.title: Using a vial on the barrel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure what this stuff is. I had better be VERY careful with it; I had better not drop it either...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what this is?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Where did you get this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: From one of the barrels at the digsite.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: This is a VERY dangerous liquid called nitroglycerin. Be careful how you handle it. Don't drop it or it will explode!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what this powder is?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Really, you do find the most unusual items. I know what this is, it's a strong chemical called ammonium nitrate. Why you want this I'll never know...

sections.title: Adding the arcenia root to the mixed chemicals

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excellent! This looks just right!

sections.title: Using a tinderbox on the bricks before pouring the mixture on them

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, what am I trying to achieve here?

sections.title: Using a rock pick on the bricks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That would be like cutting the lawn with nail scissors! It would take a year to chip away these rocks!

sections.title: Using a pickaxe on the bricks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was a good idea, but these blocks are huge. I' m going to need something much more powerful to shift these...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, the mixture is all over the bricks. I need some way to ignite this compound.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whoa! This is going to blow! I'd better run!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that was a big explosion! What's that noise I can hear? Sounds like bones moving or something...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It says: Tremble mortal, before the altar of our dread lord Zaros.

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: I hear you've been given access to the private mining shafts. You're very lucky.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to find something... interesting.

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Oh? What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A talisman linked to Zaros, so Terry says.

lines.speaker: Researcher
lines.text: Wow! That's a serious find! Good luck in the lower digs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found this in a hidden cavern beneath the digsite.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Incredible!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is an altar down there. The place is crawling with skeletons!

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Yuck!

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: This is an amazing discovery! All this while we were convinced that no other race had lived here.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: It seems the followers of Saradomin have tried to cover up the evidence of the Zaros altar. This whole city must have been built over it!

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Thanks for your help; your sharp eyes have spotted what many have missed. Here, take this gold as your reward.
